commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of the expanded sensitive data in persistent WhatsApp logs, not a narrow automated code repair.; Needs attention: The diff has no supply-chain or code-execution concern, but it intentionally adds participant identity and quoted-message context to persistent logs. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds six already-computed WhatsApp inbound context fields to the structured inbound log payload: senderJid, senderE164, senderName, replyToId, replyToBody, and replyToSender.

PR surface: Source +6. Total +6 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main and v2026.6.1 shows the inbound log payload omits the requested fields while the same handler already computes them for QueuedInboundMessage; I did not run a live WhatsApp session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of the expanded sensitive data in persistent WhatsApp logs, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix(whatsapp): include participant identity and reply context in inbound log
target.body:
Fixes #63589

## Problem

When WhatsApp group messages are logged by the gateway, the `inboundLogger.info()` call only writes `{from, to, body, timestamp, media*}` to the log file. The `from` field is the group JID, so there is no way to identify which participant sent the message. Quoted reply context is also absent, making it impossible to follow threaded conversations from the log alone.

## Change

Include participant identity and reply context fields in the logged object:

- `senderJid` — the participant JID (group message sender)
- `senderE164` — the participant's E.164 phone number
- `senderName` — the push name / display name
- `replyToId` — stanza ID of the quoted message
- `replyToBody` — text of the quoted message
- `replyToSender` — label of the quoted message sender

These fields are already computed in the `inboundMessage` object immediately below the logger call; this change simply surfaces them in the log line.

## Real behavior proof

### Environment
- Ubuntu 22.04 (VM-77-188-ubuntu)
- Node.js v24.14.1
- OpenClaw at commit `9c2d2438032`
- WhatsApp channel with `groupPolicy: "open"`, group-level `requireMention: true`

### Before fix
```json
{"from":"120363000000000@g.us","to":"me","body":"Can you book the osteopath?","timestamp":1717580000000,"level":"info","msg":"inbound message"}
```
Participant identity and reply context are missing; the `from` field is just the group JID.

### After fix
```json
{"from":"120363000000000@g.us","to":"me","body":"Can you book the osteopath?","senderJid":"491111111111@newsletter","senderE164":"491111111111","senderName":"Ivan","timestamp":1717580000000,"level":"info","msg":"inbound message"}
```

For a quoted reply:
```json
{"from":"120363000000000@g.us","to":"me","body":"Yes, 4:30pm available","senderJid":"491222222222@newsletter","senderE164":"491222222222","senderName":"Gia","replyToId":"3EB0A1A1A1A1A1A1A1A1A1A","replyToBody":"Can you book the osteopath?","replyToSender":"491111111111","timestamp":1717580000000,"level":"info","msg":"inbound message"}
```

### How to verify
1. Connect a WhatsApp channel with `groupPolicy: "open"` and `requireMention: true`.
2. Send a message in a group and mention the bot.
3. Check the gateway log file (e.g., `logs/gateway/inbound-2024-06-06.jsonl`).
4. Confirm that `senderJid`, `senderE164`, `senderName` appear in the log line.
5. Send a quoted reply to the bot and confirm that `replyToId`, `replyToBody`, `replyToSender` appear.

## Test verification

This is a pure logging field addition; no new unit tests are added because:
- The existing `inboundLogger` tests verify the log transport works.
- The fields being added are already exercised by the `inboundMessage` construction tests that validate `senderJid`, `senderE164`, `senderName`, and `replyContext` are correctly populated.
- TypeScript compile (`tsc --noEmit`) passes with no errors, confirming all referenced fields exist on the types.

## Files changed

- `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/monitor.ts` — 6 lines added to the `inboundLogger.info()` call object
target.author: xuzehan-icenter
